title: Shiny Dollers (Joke)
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Object $: “Shiny Dollers” (Joke)}} {{Joke}}{{Notice|body=This page was designed as a companion page to “[[Shiny Nickles (Joke)|Shiny Nickles]]”.}}
{{Image Banner|image=Manydollars.webp}} At first glance, '''Shiny Dollers''' appear to be a laminated $1 dollar bill. Yet their looks deceive you. Shiny Dollers have been polished to a bright shine, making them worth $1.50 instead of the traditional $1 dollar bill. When they were first minted in 1975, the president declared them to be destroyed, as if they were put into circulation, it would cause extreme inflation. Following this, on July 4th, 1976, the Shiny Dollers were dumped into the backrooms in the “Great Doller Transit” When the Shiny Dollers were first discovered in the backrooms, they were immediately adopted as currency. Like the previous Shiny Nickles, which had a finite amount, only 2,587 Shiny Dollers were discovered. Even when the backrooms was caught up in the Shiny Nickle craze, they still found a way to drag the Shiny Dollers into it, and entire wars were fought over two or three Shiny Dollers. Casinos at the time didn’t want to accept such a currency, as it would attract unwanted attention from street gangs and criminals looking to steal the Shiny Dollers. The masses pushed and pushed and pushed to have Shiny Dollers accepted at casinos and even advocated for a Shiny Doller area, specifically dedicated to having machines and tables where only Shiny Dollers were accepted. Eventually, the people of the backrooms threatened to stop going to the casinos altogether, and then the casinos cracked under pressure. {{Image Banner|image=nomoredollars.jpeg}} You and others flooded into the casino on opening day, with your life savings of two Shiny Dollers. But, Tyche did not smile upon you that fateful day. You gambled all your Shiny Dollers away on a Blackjack hand. You stayed with 14. The next card was a 7. Eventually by the end of the day, everyone had lost all their Shiny Dollers to the casino. The backrooms turned back to Shiny Nickles, but they had fallen that day as well. The backrooms had to devolve back to a simple bartering system, just like the day of its inception. But out of nowhere, everything changed in a backrooms-wide upset. On March 1st, 1984, a former employee of the casino came forward in a video posted on YouTube. The video was 30 hours long, carefully going through all the unfair tricks the casino implemented in all the slot machines, roulette wheels, and even decks of cards. When the gamblers who had lost their dollers and nickles that day discovered the video, they were instantly overcome with rage. They all had thought their nickles and dollers were lost fairly that day. Avarice blinded them, they assumed. But no. Their money was whisked away by Mr. Cash ₤ Moolah (Yes, ₤ '''is''' his middle name) All the gamblers, enraged by this diabolical act of pure greed, scoured the backrooms for any sign of him, cluttering up the bounds of reality. And when they did, they showed no mercy in destroying him, and even his huge mansion, making sure not even any rubble or shreds of his exorbitantly priced clothes remained. {{Image Banner|image=nomoremoney.jpeg}}But avarice had blinded them yet again. In the frenzy of death and destruction, they had failed to notice one thing. They had broken Mr. Moolah’s remote. It was the only thing that could open up the virtually impenetrable vault where all their Nickles and Dollers now permanently resided. The gamblers flew into another fit of rage, but soon realized it was meaningless. Many of the enraged gamblers attempted to develop other currencies to replace their dollers and nickles, such as Shiny Nickle Crypto, Shiny Penies, Dirty Nickles, and even Creased Dollers, nothing ever managed to take the place of the nickles and dollers. Now, the vault forever stands as a reminder of the greed humans exhibit when they find something their mind makes them feel an everlasting sense of ''want'' for. The gamblers neglected to destroy the casino. Instead, Mr. Moolah’s grandson took control and modified all the slot machines and even the tables to accept Almond Water as a new form of currency. Yet again, there’s no lesson here. We just live in a society. {{Collapse|num=2|initial='''> Open Author Information'''|final='''> Close Author Information'''|body= '''Author''': [[User:Nicksquid|squid]]. '''Original Inspiration''': [[Shiny Nickles (Joke)]] by [[User:PortulTrademark|Portul]]. }}
